title: Diff lines — background-tinted red/green with EL-to-end-of-line
task:
Today's diff render uses foreground-coloured `+ ` and `- ` prefixes (red/green text on default background). Codex / git's `--color-moved` / GitHub's diff view all use BACKGROUND tinting — a soft red wash across the removed line, soft green across the added line. Reads as a whole-line gestalt at a glance, not as 'spot the plus sign.'

OUTCOME — render_diff produces lines where the BACKGROUND is tinted: soft dark red (around 60,20,20 RGB or palette equivalent) for removed lines, soft dark green (20,50,20) for added. After the content, emit EL (Erase in Line, CSI K) so the background colour paints to the end of the row regardless of content width. Foreground stays as today's syntect-highlighted text. The `+ ` / `- ` prefix can stay (still useful for copy-paste / for non-true-colour terminals) OR be removed in favour of pure background — Tom's preference.

requirements.description: Modify render_diff in crates/norn-tui/src/render/content.rs to emit background-coloured lines for removed/added. Use SGR 48;2;R;G;B for true-colour (caps.true_colour=true) and SGR 48;5;N for 256-colour palette (caps.true_colour=false — pick palette indices that approximate the soft red/green). Define two new constants: `DIFF_BG_REMOVED` and `DIFF_BG_ADDED` as RgbColor values, then add a `bg_for(colour, caps)` helper alongside `colour_for` in render/style.rs (or inline if simpler). Emit `\x1b[K` (EL) after the line content and before the line-ending `\n` so the background paints to end-of-row.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test: a Diff block with removed='let x = 1;' produces output containing the SGR background-set sequence (48;2; or 48;5; depending on caps), the content, and `\x1b[K` before the newline. The line in true-colour mode contains `48;2;` followed by three RGB numbers. cargo test -p norn-tui passes.

requirements.description: Decision flag: keep the `+ ` / `- ` prefix or remove it. RECOMMENDED: keep, but render it DIM (`\x1b[2m...\x1b[22m`) so the eye reads the background colour as the primary signal and the prefix as supporting metadata. Rationale: copy-paste hostility goes down if the line still works as a unified-diff line, but visual noise goes down because the prefix doesn't fight the background colour. Document this choice as a code comment near the render.
requirements.acceptance: The `- ` / `+ ` prefix is rendered dim and the line background colour spans the full row.

requirements.description: Reset behaviour matters. After EL fills the line, the SGR background MUST be reset BEFORE the newline (or right after EL) so the NEXT line doesn't inherit. Test: a Diff with three removed + two added lines produces output where each line resets its background SGR before \n. The line after the diff (whatever comes next in the body) does NOT carry over the background.
requirements.acceptance: Unit test asserts that for sequential lines, each line's SGR-bg-reset (\x1b[49m or \x1b[0m) precedes the line's terminating \n.

requirements.description: Gates pass: cargo check -p norn-tui; cargo clippy -p norn-tui --all-targets -- -D warnings; cargo test -p norn-tui; grep TODO/FIXME/HACK in changed files returns zero.

context:
FILES:
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/content.rs — render_diff (line 124).
- crates/norn-tui/src/render/style.rs — colour_for, bg/fg helpers.
- crates/norn-tui/src/terminal/caps.rs — TerminalCaps. true_colour field gates 24-bit vs 256-palette.

WHAT'S ALREADY CORRECT — don't reinvent:
- The per-side syntect highlighting (call site preserved — just wrap with background-colour SGR before).
- The `removed.lines()` / `added.lines()` iteration with the no-trailing-newline fix from the previous bug.
- The ContentBlock::Diff data structure.

SGR REFERENCE:
- SGR 48;2;R;G;B — set background to RGB (true-colour).
- SGR 48;5;N — set background to palette index N (256-colour).
- SGR 49 — reset background to default (preferred over full SGR 0 reset which would also kill the foreground).
- CSI K (\x1b[K) — erase from cursor to end of line, inheriting the current background colour.

DESIGN TASTE: low-saturation, dark-side backgrounds. The aim is not 'oh look at this red strip' but 'this line lives in a different visual zone.' Lift the line out of the body without screaming. Codex uses around (40,15,15) for removed and (15,40,15) for added on a dark theme — those numbers are a good starting point.

STANDARDS: no unwrap/expect/panic/todo; pedantic clippy clean; no TODO/FIXME/HACK; files under 500 LOC.
